_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of organizational information technology (IT) user security policies (e.g., account creation, password rules, access control).
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of data policy authoring, including policy structure standards, enforcement mechanisms, exception handling processes, and policy lifecycle management.
comments: DCWF ksa superset of WIDAI DG domain. DG-K-008 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DA-K-012 (DA, cosine: 0.575), SP-K-006 (SP, cosine: 0.575).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.4/10 (raw 0.441). Shared vocabulary: [knowledge]. DCWF-only terms [access, account, control, creation, information, organizational] vs WIDAI-only terms [authoring, data, enforcement, exception, handling, lifecycle] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.877 (P=0.868, R=0.886). Balanced P/R — the 16-word DCWF element and 18-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (62.0%). Despite 'Superset of' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Knowledge of organizational information technology (IT) user security policies (" and "Knowledge of data policy authoring, including policy structure standards, enforc". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.622 > STS 0.441 (gap +0.181). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [knowledge] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 6.5%. Shared tokens: [knowledge]. DCWF-only: [access, account, control, creation, information, organizational]. WIDAI-only: [authoring, data, enforcement, exception, handling, lifecycle].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 62.0% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.
STS 0.441 barely clears classification threshold for Superset of. This match is fragile and may indicate insufficient KSA coverage for this competency area.

Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.181 with low STS 0.441. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
